items.caption: A diagram illustrating the concept of a vernier scale, which is a type of scale used in precision instruments. the diagram features a main scale with increments labeled from 0 to 50, and a vernier scale with increments labeled from 0 to 10. the vernier scale is superimposed on the main scale, with the zero of the vernier scale aligned with the 10 on the main scale. the text in the image explains that the vernier scale divides the last unit on the main scale into another set of fractional parts, and that the main scale is read at the zero of the vernier scale. the image also includes a question asking what the reading is for the vernier scale, with the answer provided as 26.5°. the text also mentions that mechanical instruments use vernier scales to improve the precision of the instrument.
